channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Utah just approved a data center twice the size of Manhattan that will consume more electricity than the entire state
item.description: A data center twice the size of Manhattan that will consume around 9GW of power has just been approved in Utah.

item.title: Meta brings virtual writing to everyone with Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses
item.description: Meta is rolling out new features to its Meta Ray-Ban Display smart glasses, including bringing the ability to write messages just with hand gestures to all users. You'll be able to use the feature in WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram and with "native Android and iOS messaging," Meta says. The feature, which relies on the Meta Ray-Ban […]

item.title: We Now Know How Many People the CDC Is Monitoring for Hantavirus
item.description: There are no confirmed cases in the US, but 41 people who were potentially exposed to the Andes virus are in quarantine or being monitored for symptoms.

item.title: I hate expensive headphones — but after years of testing, I've finally found a pair I can't live without
item.description: I've been these AKG headphones for 12 months now, and they still get daily use.

item.title: Behold, the Elon Musk jackass trophy
item.description: Yesterday, in Musk v. Altman, before the jurors came in, Sam Altman's team passed up what looked - from a distance - like a little league trophy. It was not. Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers had the lawyers read the inscription aloud for the press: "Never stop being a jackass." It's a commemoration OpenAI employees bought for […]

item.title: OpenAI says Codex is coming to your phone
item.description: The update gives users enhanced flexibility over how they can manage their workflows.

item.title: Meta is bringing third-party apps and games to its display glasses
item.description: The $800 smart glasses could soon be a lot more useful.

item.title: Leaked images reveal Xbox Elite 3 controller with mysterious new buttons
item.description: Hours after a smaller Xbox Cloud Gaming controller appeared online, Brazil's Anatel regulator has also accidentally published images of what appears to be Microsoft's upcoming Xbox Elite 3 controller. Tecnoblog has posted images that show the successor to the Elite 2 controller, with an interchangeable D-Pad, paddles, and two new mysterious buttons. The Elite 3 […]...

item.title: Lovable just backed a company that’s looking to bring vibe coding to hardware
item.description: Hardware company Atech raised $800,000 in pre-seed funding, including from a16z’s scout fund, Sequoia Scout Fund, and Nordic Makers.

item.title: Sony Xperia 1 VIII : ce post X est-il une bourde ou un coup de génie
item.description: Sony a publié sur X une comparaison « avant / après » de l'IA photo du Xperia 1 VIII où l'image « après » est visiblement moins belle. Qu'est-ce qui s'est vraiment passé ?

item.title: 'The second life of drones': Why thousands of UAVs in Ukraine have stopped working, how a team of 'craftsmen' is fixing 24,000 obsolete drones every year, and what it means for the future of the drone industry
item.description: Ukraine is rebuilding obsolete drones after electronic warfare rapidly rendered thousands of freshly delivered UAVs ineffective across frontline combat zones.

item.title: An Engineer’s Post Protesting Laptop Surveillance Is Going Viral Inside Meta
item.description: Meta employees in the US and UK are organizing against corporate software that tracks workers’ keystrokes and mouse activity.

item.title: Amazon hurled its Lord of the Rings MMO back into the fires of Mount Doom
item.description: It's still making a new game set in Middle-earth, though.

item.title: Cerebras IPO makes billions for Benchmark but VC Eric Vishria almost didn’t take the meeting
item.description: Benchmark almost never backs hardware startups. So Eric Vishria dragged his feet ten years ago before agreeing to hear Cerebras' pitch.

item.title: Agent authorization is broken — and authentication passing makes it worse
item.description: Anthony Grieco, Cisco’s SVP and chief security and trust officer, did not hesitate when VentureBeat asked whether rogue agent incidents are reaching Cisco’s customer base."A hundred percent. We see them regularly," Grieco told VentureBeat in an exclusive interview at RSAC 2026. "I've heard some that I can't repeat, but they do get to the places of, you know, agents are doing things that they think are the right things to do."The incidents Grieco described follow a consistent pattern: authentication passes, identity checks clear. The agent is exactly who it claims to be. Then it accesses data i...

item.title: Best Early Memorial Day Mattress Deals: Helix, Saatva (2026)
item.description: It’s one of the best times of the year to buy a mattress, and deals on our favorite models have already started.

item.title: Don't wait for Memorial Day to buy a new TV — shop record-low prices right now, with up to $1,400 off 4K, QLED, and OLED TVs
item.description: There's no need to wait for Memorial Day; you can shop impressive TV deals right now, including up to $1,400 off the best-rated 4K, QLED, and OLED TVs.

item.title: OpenAI’s Codex is now in the ChatGPT mobile app
item.description: OpenAI is going to let users access Codex, its desktop AI tool that can write code and use apps on your computer, from the ChatGPT app on your phone. Following the surge in popularity for Anthropic's Claude Code, OpenAI has been working quickly to try and catch up, including by cutting back on "side quests," […]

item.title: “If you’re manufacturing EVs that can’t be repaired, it’s going to be a real challenge to sustain residual values” — experts call for more repairability in the battery industry
item.description: Experts are calling for more EV repairability, as range-focussed battery packs could seriously hurt residual values

item.title: OpenAI brings its Codex coding app to mobile
item.description: The integration allows you to keep tabs on your coding projects on the go.

item.title: What happens when AI starts building itself?
item.description: Richard Socher's new $650 million startup wants to build an AI that can research and improve itself indefinitely — and he insists it will actually ship products.

item.title: Netflix wants to use generative AI to make animated shorts
item.description: Sigh.

item.title: Meta’s summer sale drops Ray-Ban’s new smart glasses to record-low prices
item.description: If you’ve been curious about Ray-Ban’s Meta smart glasses, right now they’re on sale in Meta’s biggest deal event outside Black Friday. As part of the company’s Summer Sale event, which runs through May 26th, the original model is on sale starting at $224.25 ($74 off) at Amazon, Best Buy, and directly from Meta, matching […]

item.title: Procès OpenAI | Elon Musk absent, Sam Altman au premier rang
item.description: Trois semaines d’audiences à Oakland (Californie) et la trajectoire d’OpenAI en jeu : les créateurs de ChatGPT sont attentifs jeudi aux plaidoiries finales du procès intenté par Elon Musk, dernier acte avant que le jury ne se retire pour délibérer.

item.title: The VW ID.Buzz is returning for model year 2027 with a new trim for camping
item.description: The Tourer model features a fold-out mattress, removable window binds and more.

item.title: The HDMI ARC port on your TV has a very specific job
item.description: The distinction between various flavors of HDMI can get a bit technical.
